title: hotato rubric evaluation, schema rubric.v1
description: The output of `hotato rubric run` (and the conversation-test `assertions.rubric` lane): a user-authored rubric criterion evaluated against a call's transcript / tool-trace by a pinned model. HONESTY INVARIANT, structural: every result carries `deterministic: false` with FULL provenance (model + model_digest, prompt id/version/sha256, temperature, input_sha256, cache_key, cached, votes, disagreement, confidence, citations). This is a SEPARATE schema from assert.v1 -- a rubric result is NEVER an assert.v1 result and NEVER carries deterministic:true. `summary` splits pass/fail/inconclusive/error and NEVER emits a merged percentage or an `overall_score`. A rubric result is ADVISORY by default; a run opts into gating with --gate. Missing/insufficient evidence -> INCONCLUSIVE (never a fabricated verdict). Additive-only: new provenance fields may appear without a version bump; a consumer must ignore unknown fields.

exit_code.description: Advisory by default: 0 regardless of rubric verdicts. With --gate, 1 if any result's status is FAIL. INCONCLUSIVE/ERROR stay advisory (a model that could not decide, or a judge backend that was unreachable, never silently gates by itself). 2 is a usage error raised before any result exists.

advisory.description: True unless --gate was passed. When advisory, a rubric FAIL is reported but never affects exit_code -- the model-judged lane never silently blocks a release.

gated.description: True when --gate was passed: a rubric FAIL then gates exit_code like a deterministic FAIL.

deterministic.description: HONESTY INVARIANT: always false for a rubric result -- a model backed this verdict, so it is never conflated with an assert.v1 deterministic:true result and never counted in the deterministic summary.

status.description: PASS/FAIL: a decisive, aggregated model verdict. INCONCLUSIVE: insufficient evidence, a non-unanimous or low-confidence vote, or an abstention -- never a guess. ERROR: the judge backend failed (e.g. unreachable); honest, advisory.

dimension.description: OPTIONAL. The report dimension this rubric result is grouped under, copied from the rubric's `dimension` tag. A grouping key only -- never a weight, never part of any blended number.

rationale.description: The model's short explanation for the verdict, grounded in the cited evidence. Never a score.

model_digest.description: The model's content digest from the backend (Ollama /api/tags), pinning exactly which weights judged. Null only when the backend could not report one (then status is ERROR/INCONCLUSIVE).

provider.description: The judge backend: 'ollama' (local, default, zero egress), 'hosted' (opt-in egress), or 'fake' (test double).

input_sha256.description: sha256 over the canonical evidence + rubric input the verdict was computed from.

cache_key.description: sha256(provider:model + prompt_sha256 + input_sha256) -- the content address a cache hit is byte-identical under.

cached.description: True if this verdict was replayed byte-identically from the content-addressed cache; false if freshly computed.

verdict_sha256.description: sha256 of the stored verdict record (excluding runtime cached/drift fields). A cache hit reproduces the same verdict_sha256 -- that is the exact, scoped reproducibility claim (cached replay is byte-identical; the model itself is NOT claimed deterministic).

votes.description: The raw per-repetition categorical votes, before aggregation.

disagreement.description: True if the votes were not unanimous.

confidence.description: The majority-vote fraction across repetitions. Not a calibrated probability -- the plain agreement rate of the votes.

citations.description: Citations to the exact evidence the verdict rests on: transcript turns (turn index + short quote) and/or trace events (span id).

signer.description: OPTIONAL: the {key_id, algo} of an Ed25519/HMAC 'judge-record' signature over the verdict, when the operator opted in.

signature.description: OPTIONAL: hex signature proving the cached verdict is unmutated.

drift.description: OPTIONAL (only on a --no-cache re-query when a cached verdict existed): a diff surfacing that the fresh verdict differs from the cached one. Never hidden.

input_refs.description: sha256 digests of the exact evidence supplied (transcript, trace) so a verdict is bound to what it judged.

review.description: Human-review status. `human_required` is set when the rubric's review.human_required_on triggers fire (a FAIL, a disagreement, or confidence below the threshold). A model-judged result stays ADVISORY until a human reviews it; it never forges a human label.

rubric_object.description: The USER-AUTHORED rubric: what to judge and how strictly. Never a default -- a rubric verdict is invalid without a cited, versioned rubric object.

kind.description: judge_rubric: a model judges it. human_rubric: a human must judge it -- the model NEVER scores a human_rubric item; it stays INCONCLUSIVE with human_required until a person decides.

criterion.description: The natural-language question the judge decides, e.g. 'Did the agent acknowledge the caller's frustration before proposing a fix?'

evidence.description: Which evidence the judge may read. Missing required evidence -> INCONCLUSIVE, never a guess.
